query: Magnificent 7 tech companies (Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla) large acquisitions $10 billion or more announced or rumored in 2026, especially mid-2026. Any pending big M&A deals as of June-July 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of Magnificent 7 large acquisitions ($10B+) in 2026:

## Key Findings

**Confirmed $10B+ deals by Mag 7 companies:**

1. **Alphabet/Google → Wiz — $32B** (largest in Google's history; **CLOSED March 11, 2026** after DOJ + EU approval)

2. **Amazon → Globalstar — ~$11.6B** (announced ~April 14, 2026; **still PENDING as of mid-2026**). Amazon is also acquiring Apple's 20% stake in Globalstar. This is the **one live, pending $10B+ Mag 7 acquisition** in the June–July 2026 window you asked about.

3. **Nvidia → Groq — $20B** (announced late Dec 2025, but structured as a licensing/acqui-hire to avoid antitrust; under Senate scrutiny)

**The big rumored deal — Tesla–SpaceX merger:** Heavily rumored but **NOT officially announced** as of July 12, 2026. SpaceX's ~June 12 IPO (~$2.2T market cap vs Tesla's ~$1.4T) fueled speculation. Analysts split: Wedbush's Dan Ives put odds >80% over the next year, while Polymarket priced an official announcement at only ~24.5%. (Note: SpaceX–xAI already merged in Feb 2026 at ~$1.25T, but that's Musk's private entities, not a Mag 7 acquirer.)

**Sub-threshold:** Meta made smaller AI buys (Manus ~$2B, Assured Robot Intelligence undisclosed). Meta's "$27B Nebius" and "$21B CoreWeave" figures are compute contracts, not acquisitions. Apple, Microsoft, and Tesla did not announce any $10B+ acquisitions in 2026.

**Context:** The hyperscalers are pouring ~$650–700B+ into AI capex in 2026 rather than M&A, and the year's biggest overall deals (Netflix-WBD $83B, UP-Norfolk Southern $85B, EA $55B) sit outside the Mag 7.

The full structured findings with all sources and data points have been recorded.

Megadeals Power Tech M&A Surge Toward $4 Trillion in 2026

key_facts: Alphabet/Google closed its $32B all-cash acquisition of cloud-security firm Wiz on March 11, 2026 — the largest deal in Google's history — after DOJ and EU approval.
Amazon announced a ~$11.6B cash-and-stock acquisition of satellite operator Globalstar (~April 14, 2026); still pending as of mid-2026.
As part of the Globalstar deal, Amazon is acquiring Apple's 20% stake and voting rights (revealed ~May 27–28, 2026); Apple remains a partner via its Emergency SOS satellite feature.
Nvidia's $20B Groq deal (announced late Dec 2025) was structured as a non-exclusive licensing/acqui-hire to avoid antitrust review; Senators Warren and Blumenthal opened an inquiry on March 20, 2026.
A Tesla–SpaceX merger was heavily rumored but NOT officially announced as of July 12, 2026.
SpaceX IPO'd ~June 12, 2026 (~$85.7B offering, ~$2.2T market cap vs Tesla's ~$1.4T), which analysts say makes a SpaceX-led Tesla acquisition more feasible.
Polymarket priced the probability of an official Tesla–SpaceX merger announcement at ~24.5% as of July 12, 2026; the June 30 deadline passed with no announcement.
SpaceX acquired xAI in February 2026 (~$1.25T combined valuation) — a Musk-empire deal, not a Magnificent 7 public-company acquisition.
Meta's 2026 acquisitions were sub-$10B: Manus (~$2B+, AI agents) and Assured Robot Intelligence (undisclosed, humanoid robotics).
Meta's '$27B Nebius' and '$21B CoreWeave' figures are AI compute/cloud-capacity contracts, not acquisitions.
PwC 2026 mid-year outlook: global tech M&A heading toward ~$4 trillion, strongest since 2021, with megadeals >$5B driving nearly half of value.
The four hyperscalers (Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft) are prioritizing AI capex (~$650–700B+ in 2026) over acquisitions.
Largest overall 2026 deals are outside the Mag 7: Netflix–Warner Bros. Discovery $83B, Union Pacific–Norfolk Southern $85B, PIF/Silver Lake–EA $55B, Charter–Cox $34.5B.

data_points.metric: Apple stake in Globalstar being transferred to Amazon
Polymarket probability of official Tesla–SpaceX merger announcement
